reason: The knowledge file omits `:必須` from the base spec of 携帯電話番号(加入), but the source RST itself is internally contradictory: the base spec says "必須" (unconditionally required) while the notes say it must be blank when 市外 is not entered. The knowledge file resolves this contradiction by preserving only the conditional logic from the notes, which is the more specific and operationally correct interpretation. An AI reading the knowledge file would correctly understand the full conditional validation rule: required when 市外 is entered, must be blank otherwise. No validation case is lost or misrepresented.

findings_assessment.location: s3 — ファイル情報 / データレコード / 携帯電話番号(加入) 説明列
findings_assessment.justification: The source RST contains an internal contradiction: the base spec marks 携帯電話番号(加入) as `:必須` (unconditionally required), but the accompanying notes specify it must be blank when 携帯電話番号(市外) is not entered — making unconditional required impossible. The knowledge file resolves this contradiction by retaining only the conditional logic from the notes, which fully covers both validation cases (required when 市外 is present; must be blank when absent). An AI agent reading the knowledge file will not misunderstand the validation requirement because all actionable cases are represented. The omission of the contradictory `:必須` tag actually improves coherence. Phase E did not apply a fix because the current description is functionally complete and adding `:必須` back alongside the conditional 'must be blank' rule would introduce an explicit logical contradiction into the knowledge file.
